step.title: Extraction and Structuring
step.goal: Transform analysis into a structured knowledge document that's reusable, actionable, and consumable by both humans and agents.

substep.title: Choose Output Structure
substep.action:
Based on knowledge type, select appropriate structure:
**patterns** → Pattern Catalog - Pattern name, problem, solution, examples, trade-offs
**conventions** → Convention Reference - Rule, rationale, examples (good/bad), exceptions
**templates** → Template with Instructions - Template content, placeholder descriptions, usage guide
**guidelines** → Decision Guide - When to use, how to decide, examples, anti-patterns
**vocabulary** → Glossary - Term, definition, usage, related terms
**remediation** → Remediation Set - Per item: source file, the defect, the exact change (diff-shaped)

substep.title: Create Knowledge Document
substep.action:
Generate the knowledge document with this structure:
```markdown # {{knowledge_name}}
## Overview Brief description of what this knowledge covers.
## When to Use Situations where this knowledge applies.
## {{Main Content Section}} (Structure depends on knowledge type)
## Examples Concrete examples showing patterns in action.
## Anti-Patterns What NOT to do and why.
## References Source material this was extracted from. ```

substep.title: Write Pattern Entries
substep.action:
For each pattern, document:
### {{pattern.name}}
**Problem:** What situation triggers this pattern?
**Solution:** How the pattern addresses the problem.
**Structure:** ``` (Show the pattern's skeleton/template) ```
**Example:** ``` (Concrete implementation from source) ```
**Rationale:** Why this approach works.
**Trade-offs:** What you gain/lose by using this pattern.
**When NOT to Use:** Situations where this pattern is wrong.

substep.title: Write Convention Entries
substep.action:
For each convention, document:
### {{convention.name}}
**Rule:** Clear statement of the convention.
**Rationale:** Why this convention exists.
**Good Example:** ``` (Correct usage) ```
**Bad Example:** ``` (Incorrect usage) ```
**Exceptions:** When the rule doesn't apply.

substep.title: Write Template
substep.action:
Create the template with:
## Template: {{template_name}}
### Usage When and how to use this template.
### Template ``` {{template_content_with_placeholders}} ```
### Placeholders | Placeholder | Description | Example | |-------------|-------------|---------| | {{name}} | What goes here | Example value |
### Customization What can/should be modified vs kept as-is.

substep.title: Write Remediation Set
substep.action:
For each localized target produce a diff-shaped entry the user can approve before any write:
### {{target.source_file}} **Defect:** what's wrong here. **Change:** the exact edit (old → new) — behavioral, not vague. **Prevention check:** is this eliminating the error class at its source, or just adding a downstream check? Prefer the former (per key-responsibility).

substep.title: Add Anti-Patterns
substep.action:
Document what NOT to do:
## Anti-Patterns
### {{anti_pattern_name}} **What it looks like:** Description of the mistake. **Why it's wrong:** What problems it causes. **Instead:** What to do instead.

substep.title: Review Extracted Knowledge

rules.rule: Every pattern needs at least one concrete example
Include anti-patterns - knowing what NOT to do is valuable
Keep entries scannable - use headers, bullets, code blocks

section.title: LLM-Friendly Formatting
section.description: Format knowledge for effective LLM consumption:

rules.rule: Use XML tags for structure (easy to parse)
Use emphasis keywords appropriately (MUST, NEVER, etc.)
Keep items self-contained (can be loaded independently)

section.example: <pattern name="error-handling"> <problem>API operations can fail</problem> <solution>Wrap in try/catch, return structured error</solution> <example>...</example> <rule>ALWAYS log before returning error</rule> </pattern>

section.title: Actionability Test
section.description: Good extracted knowledge passes this test:
section.test:
Could someone unfamiliar with the source material: 1. Understand when this applies? 2. Follow the pattern/convention correctly? 3. Avoid common mistakes? 4. Adapt it to their specific situation?
If any answer is "no", add more context/examples.

section.title: Output Formats by Type
section.format:
```markdown # Pattern: Name
**Problem:** When you need to... **Solution:** Use this approach... **Structure:** (template)
**Example:** (concrete code/content)
**Trade-offs:** Gains X, costs Y ```

```markdown ## Convention: Name
**Rule:** Always/Never do X **Rationale:** Because...
Good: (example) Bad: (example)
**Exceptions:** Unless... ```

```markdown ## Template: Name
### Usage Use when...
### Template (content with {{placeholders}})
### Placeholders | Name | Description | ```
